free_text: Extension of excitation functions up to 50 MeV for
activation products in deuteron irradiations of Pr
and Tm targets
Targets were Tm and Pr oxides, Tm2O3 and Pr6O11.
The Tm2O3 50-70um
thick in Tm equivalent, purity 99.9% and Pr6O11 25-60um
thick in Pr equivalent, purity 99.5% targets were
prepared using a sedimentation technique on Al backings
of 100um and covered with 10um Al.
7 Pr and 8 Tm target in a stack with Al energy
degraders were irradiated for 2500s with a constant,
continuously monitored, beam current of 50nA.
After comparing the initial beam parameters with the
one deduced from monitor reactions no change in nominal
incident energy and a small correction in particle flux
was needed, final beam current 52.5 nA.
For activity measurement no chemical separation was
used.
Decay data were taken from NUDAT2.6 online database.
The uncertainty on the median energy in the
foils includes the cumulative effects of uncertainty of
incident beam energy, uncertainty of stopping power of
each foil due to thickness variation and  straggling
effects. Uncertainty of the primary incident beam was
+- 0.2MeV which increased up to +-0.8MeV in the last
foil of the stack.
The uncertainty on the cross section values
are estimated in the  standard way as the square root
of the quadratic summation of the independently
contributing parameters. Uncertainty of time parameters
is not included. Overall uncertainty were 11-19%.
surface density of target nuclei(7%),
incident charged particle flux (7%),
detector efficiency (5%)
nuclear decay and branching data(4%)
statistical and peak fitting (1-13%)
The experimental data were compared to the
results of the theoretical model codes TENDL.
The measured experimental data were compared to
those found in the literature.
Fitted excitation functions were used for
determination of integral physical yields.
